name: Five-Day Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 1/3 cups Great Northern beans or lima beans
1/2 teaspoon salt, plus salt to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. On Day 1, cover beans with cold water and let stand for 24 hours.
2. On Day 2, drain the beans. Place them in a medium saucepan, add the bay leaf, thyme, half of the salt, and enough water to cover. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a simmer over low heat. Cook until tender (about 1 hour). Drain the beans and reserve one cup of liquid. Transfer the beans with their reserved liquid to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate.
3. On Day 3, melt butter in a large skillet over moderate heat. Add onion, carrot, and celery and sauté for 3 minutes. Add sage and cook for an additional minute. Stir in the beans with their reserved liquid and simmer for 5 minutes. Remove from heat, let cool, cover, and refrigerate for two days.
4. On Day 5, preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Butter an 11- by 13-inch baking dish. Season the beans with salt and pepper, then transfer them to the baking dish. Drizzle cream over the top and dot with bread crumbs. Bake until hot and lightly browned (15 to 20 minutes). Serve from the baking dish if desired.
